title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Incident Case: Net Cable Faults
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net cable faults increment in an evidence-backed incident resolution packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Cable Faults
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Build Net Cable Faults evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-cable-faults-1xdidm" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Cable Faults into observable performance evidence.
